+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-142
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-24_09-37-44+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-142_TASK_2026-05-24_09-30-22+0300.md — TASK packet consumed
+- docs/adr/ADR-119-real-round-fillet-substrate.md — read-only evidence
+- docs/architecture/FILLET_OUTPUT_IDENTITY.md — read-only evidence
+- crates/cad-core/src/operators/fillet/mod.rs — read-only evidence (chamfer FilletOp)
+- crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/mod.rs — read-only evidence (RoundFilletOp)
+- crates/cad-core/src/topology/resolve.rs — read-only evidence (face resolver)
+- crates/cad-core/src/topology/edge_resolve.rs — read-only evidence (edge resolver)
+- crates/cad-core/src/tessellation/mesh.rs — read-only evidence (TopologyFaceId::DEGENERATE)
+- crates/cad-projection/src/lib.rs — read-only evidence (face-id consumer)
+- crates/cad-projection/src/picking.rs — read-only evidence (selection consumer)
+- crates/cad-projection/src/render_adapter.rs — read-only evidence (highlight consumer)
+- crates/cad-projection/tests/round_fillet_brep_face_id_lookup_smoke.rs — read-only evidence (pinned DEGENERATE -> None contract)
+STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-142_TASK_2026-05-24_09-30-22+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- None. This dispatch is a read-only audit (TASK packet `MAY edit` lists only the EXEC report).
+
+### Tests
+
+- None.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None.
+
+The only artifacts produced by this dispatch are this EXEC report and the matching `.meta.json` sidecar generated by `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize`.
+
+## 5-Question D-Fillet Output-Identity Preflight Answer Block
+
+### Q1. What output identity does chamfer FilletOp have today?
+
+Chamfer `FilletOp` flows identity through the graph-level resolvers; it has no direct `BRepProvider` / `BRepEdgeProvider` impl. Concretely, in the current code:
+
+- **Graph-level face inheritance (sub-ε.α)** — `OperatorNode::Fillet` arm at `crates/cad-core/src/topology/resolve.rs:234-246` recurses to the unique upstream input and returns the upstream's `Vec<(TopologyFaceId, BRepFaceId)>` unchanged. `FilletOp::evaluate` at `crates/cad-core/src/operators/fillet/mod.rs:367-446` clones upstream `positions` and `indices` verbatim and only APPENDS two chamfer-cap triangles per filleted edge (`fillet/mod.rs:421-432`), so every upstream face still exists bit-identical in the output and identity flows through the recursion.
+
+- **Filtered edge inheritance (sub-ε.β)** — `OperatorNode::Fillet(op)` arm at `crates/cad-core/src/topology/edge_resolve.rs:189-208` recurses, then returns `upstream_edges \ op.edges()` via the `Vec::contains` filter at `edge_resolve.rs:202-207`. Filleted edges are EXCLUDED because their 2-endpoint geometry is absorbed by the chamfer; non-filleted upstream edges retain bit-identical `BRepEdgeId` byte-identity because `BRepEdgeId::for_face_pair` is shape-agnostic and face IDs inherit unchanged.
+
+- **Direct provider non-goals** — explicitly enumerated in the module-doc at `crates/cad-core/src/operators/fillet/mod.rs:30-50`: "No `impl BRepProvider for FilletOp`" and "No `impl BRepEdgeProvider for FilletOp`." The sub-ε.γ inspection halt-and-reframed cap-face provider work because all three candidate paths (cache upstream IDs, mint `KIND_FILLET_CAP` IDs, return empty Vec) tripped halt-fast guardrails; see ADR-119 Context section at `docs/adr/ADR-119-real-round-fillet-substrate.md:37-40`.
+
+- **Chamfer caps in labels/projection** — ADR-120 unparked face-label propagation: `FilletOp::evaluate` preserves upstream `face_labels` when present and pushes `TopologyFaceId::DEGENERATE` for the two appended cap triangles per filleted edge at `fillet/mod.rs:434-437`. `output_is_labeled` mirrors the input at `fillet/mod.rs:453-455`. The DEGENERATE sentinel is defined at `crates/cad-core/src/tessellation/mesh.rs:50-54`. In cad-projection, the lazy resolver `CadProjection::brep_face_id_for_triangle` at `crates/cad-projection/src/lib.rs:185-203` matches a triangle's `TopologyFaceId` against the resolver's mapping; DEGENERATE labels never match a real entry and resolve to `None`. `CadProjection::pick_face` at `crates/cad-projection/src/picking.rs:194-259` consumes the same lookup and treats `None`-resolving triangles as **transparent**, not occluding, per the LOAD-BEARING selection rule in `picking.rs:7-14`. `render_adapter::render_mesh_for` at `crates/cad-projection/src/render_adapter.rs:90-106` passes labels through as opaque `u64`s without re-deriving identity. Stable cap-face IDs are not minted; `docs/architecture/FILLET_OUTPUT_IDENTITY.md:42-55` records this as "Do chamfer caps need stable B-Rep face IDs? That remains deferred until a consumer needs to select, persist, materialize, or otherwise address chamfer-cap faces across rebuilds."
+
+### Q2. What output identity does RoundFilletOp have today?
+
+Real `RoundFilletOp` flows identity through the graph-level resolvers in parallel to chamfer (per ADR-119 D5), with the substantive differences that match ADR D2 (curved-edge inheritance) and D4 (faces retain `BRepFaceId` under face-strip removal). It also has no direct `BRepProvider` / `BRepEdgeProvider` impl. Concretely:
+
+- **Graph-level face resolver behavior (ADR D4)** — `OperatorNode::RoundFillet(_)` arm at `crates/cad-core/src/topology/resolve.rs:248-264` recurses to the unique upstream and returns the upstream's `Vec<(TopologyFaceId, BRepFaceId)>` unchanged. The arm's comment captures the rationale: identity = semantic surface, not mesh shape; faces survive face-strip-removal substitution because the affected face's triangle indices change but the surface identity does not.
+
+- **Graph-level edge resolver behavior (ADR D2)** — `OperatorNode::RoundFillet(_)` arm at `crates/cad-core/src/topology/edge_resolve.rs:210-229` recurses and returns ALL upstream edges unchanged, **including** the filleted ones. This is the substantive divergence from chamfer's filter-the-selection arm: `BRepEdgeId::for_face_pair` is shape-agnostic, so a curved edge between the same two faces retains the same byte-identity. The contract is pinned by `resolver_cuboid_then_round_fillet_inherits_all_edges_including_filleted` at `edge_resolve.rs:717-768`.
+
+- **Inherited faces/edges + nameless cylinder/cap/corner surfaces (ADR D3)** — `RoundFilletOp::evaluate` at `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/mod.rs:515-1003` clones upstream positions verbatim, substitutes filleted-edge endpoint indices in the two adjacent faces' triangles with new inset vertices (`mod.rs:1067-1101`, `mod.rs:1103-1151`), and APPENDS new geometry for cylinder surface triangles (`mod.rs:953-970`), Path-branch swept-cylinder ring stitches (`mod.rs:720-738`), and multi-edge corner-patch fan triangles (`mod.rs:1153-1207`). Every appended triangle pushes `TopologyFaceId::DEGENERATE` into `face_labels` (`round_fillet/mod.rs:733-736`, `:967-969`, `:1202-1204`). The cuboid → round-fillet projection smoke at `crates/cad-projection/tests/round_fillet_brep_face_id_lookup_smoke.rs:160-200` pins the contract: DEGENERATE cap/corner triangles resolve to `None`; inherited Cuboid labels resolve to the upstream's stable `BRepFaceId`.
+
+- **Direct provider non-goals** — explicitly enumerated in the module-doc at `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/mod.rs:52-76`: "No `impl BRepProvider for RoundFilletOp`" and "No `impl BRepEdgeProvider for RoundFilletOp`" and "No cap-face / corner-patch `BRepFaceId`; caps + corner patches remain nameless in v0 (ADR D3; pressure-deferred), and their triangles emit `TopologyFaceId::DEGENERATE`."
+
+- **`TopologyFaceId::DEGENERATE` usage** — `crates/cad-core/src/tessellation/mesh.rs:50-60` defines the sentinel as `u64::MAX` with `is_degenerate()`. Round-fillet uses it for all three appended geometries (cylinder surface, path-branch ring stitches, corner-patch fan). The picker / render-adapter / face-resolver consumers in cad-projection already treat DEGENERATE as transparent / nameless per Q1.
+
+### Q3. Which remaining gap is actually load-bearing for the next CAD product step?
+
+Each plausible identity gap is classified below. Every classification is grounded in current file/line evidence; none requires speculating about an undocumented consumer.
+
+- **Direct `BRepProvider` impl on `FilletOp` / `RoundFilletOp`** — **already-solved**. The graph-level face resolver at `crates/cad-core/src/topology/resolve.rs:234-264` provides the honest identity surface (inherited upstream IDs); both operators are explicitly identity-preserving and do not mint local face IDs. ADR-119 D5 + D6 + the sub-ε.γ halt-and-reframe (recorded at `docs/adr/ADR-119-real-round-fillet-substrate.md:37-38`) confirm direct provider impls are not the right shape.
+
+- **Direct `BRepEdgeProvider` impl on `FilletOp` / `RoundFilletOp`** — **already-solved**. The edge resolver at `crates/cad-core/src/topology/edge_resolve.rs:189-229` handles both shapes: chamfer's filter-the-selection arm and round's full-inheritance arm. Module-doc non-goals at `fillet/mod.rs:42-50` and `round_fillet/mod.rs:67-71` document this as substrate-honest.
+
+- **Stable `BRepFaceId`s for generated `RoundFilletOp` cylinder / cap / corner-patch surfaces** — **pressure-deferred**. No production consumer in the current codebase addresses these surfaces by ID: `editor/**` contains zero references to `RoundFilletOp` or `round_fillet` (verified by `grep -r RoundFillet editor/` returning no files). The single existing cad-projection consumer test at `crates/cad-projection/tests/round_fillet_brep_face_id_lookup_smoke.rs:160-200` actively asserts `None` for DEGENERATE labels and treats that as the load-bearing contract, not a defect. ADR-119 D3 at `docs/adr/ADR-119-real-round-fillet-substrate.md:90-99` is explicit: "No consumer pressure today; …doctrine-as-substrate posture: inventing identity infrastructure ahead of consumer pressure trips the validation-framework / centralization / taxonomy / extraction pressure anti-pattern."
+
+- **Stable `BRepFaceId`s for chamfer `FilletOp` cap geometry** — **pressure-deferred**. Same posture as the round case; `docs/architecture/FILLET_OUTPUT_IDENTITY.md:42-55` (the controlling doctrine doc) explicitly states the question is deferred "until a consumer needs to select, persist, materialize, or otherwise address chamfer-cap faces across rebuilds." No such consumer exists today; the projection smoke contract is `None` for DEGENERATE caps (`brep_face_id_for_triangle` semantics at `crates/cad-projection/src/lib.rs:155-203`).
+
+- **Face-label propagation for chamfer `FilletOp`** — **already-solved**. ADR-120 unparked the labeled-output case; `FilletOp::evaluate` at `crates/cad-core/src/operators/fillet/mod.rs:434-437` preserves upstream labels and emits `TopologyFaceId::DEGENERATE` for cap triangles. `output_is_labeled` mirrors the input flag at `fillet/mod.rs:453-455`. `FILLET_OUTPUT_IDENTITY.md` records this as PARTIALLY RESOLVED.
+
+- **Face-label propagation for `RoundFilletOp`** — **already-solved**. `RoundFilletOp::evaluate` at `crates/cad-core/src/operators/round_fillet/mod.rs:986-991` constructs the output `Tessellation` with labels; the face-strip-removal substitution preserves the substituted triangles' upstream labels (`mod.rs:1067-1101`) while all appended geometries (cylinder, path-branch, corner-fan) emit `TopologyFaceId::DEGENERATE` (`mod.rs:733-736`, `:967-969`, `:1202-1204`).
+
+- **Edge inheritance behavior — chamfer (filtered) vs round (full)** — **already-solved**. The two resolver arms encode the contract clearly and are pinned by unit tests at `crates/cad-core/src/topology/edge_resolve.rs:519-616` (chamfer: filter selected, retain rest byte-equal) and `edge_resolve.rs:717-822` (round: retain ALL including filleted, byte-equal). ADR-119 D2 rationale at `docs/adr/ADR-119-real-round-fillet-substrate.md:79-89` explains the semantic divergence.
+
+- **Picking / selection** — **already-solved**. `CadProjection::pick_face` at `crates/cad-projection/src/picking.rs:194-259` returns the closest *resolvable* face hit, treating DEGENERATE / unowned / topology-changing / out-of-bounds triangles as transparent. The module-level selection rule at `picking.rs:5-42` ties this directly to ADR-120 label propagation and ADR-119's nameless cap geometry. The round-fillet-specific smoke `pick_resolves_round_filleted_geometry_top_face` at `crates/cad-projection/tests/face_picking_smoke.rs:544-625` proves transparency end-to-end through a `RoundFilletOp` root.
+
+- **Projection / highlight support** — **already-solved** for inherited faces; **pressure-deferred** for cap/corner highlight. `CadProjection::face_triangle_indices` at `crates/cad-projection/src/lib.rs:238-271` already produces dense triangle indices for highlight overlays against any resolvable face. Cap-face-specific highlight would require minting cap-face IDs (the same pressure-deferred work as above) and so reduces to the cap-ID question, not a separate gap.
+
+No plausible gap is **`consumer-pressure-present`** in the current code; **none** is **`needs-ADR`** because ADR-119 (substrate + D3 nameless-in-v0 policy) and ADR-120 (chamfer face-label propagation) already pin the policy and explicitly leave the lift path open for when concrete pressure surfaces.
+
+### Q4. What is the smallest safe follow-up if the gap is bounded?
+
+The gap is **not bounded by concrete consumer pressure under existing policy**. Per Q3, every potentially-load-bearing gap is either `already-solved` or `pressure-deferred`. The smallest next step is therefore a **policy decision**, not an implementation task:
+
+> **Decision required from a human arbiter**: do not invent cap/cylinder/corner-patch stable identity work, do not add direct `BRepProvider` / `BRepEdgeProvider` impls on `FilletOp` or `RoundFilletOp`, and do not author a fresh ADR or doctrine refresh for D-Fillet output identity unless and until a concrete consumer surfaces (per-cap material assignment, cap-face selection persistence across rebuilds, per-cap LoD, per-cap highlight, scripting handle that names a cap, etc.). When such a consumer lands as real code or a green-lit product requirement, the well-scoped lift path is already documented:
+>
+> - **ADR-119 D3 lift path** (`docs/adr/ADR-119-real-round-fillet-substrate.md:96-99`): mint `BRepFaceId::KIND_FILLET_ROUND_CAP` + a `RoundFilletCapFaceTag` enum (analogous to `CuboidFaceTag` / `ExtrudeFaceTag`) + a `BRepProvider` impl on `RoundFilletOp` that returns cap-face IDs alongside the upstream-inherited IDs.
+> - **`FILLET_OUTPUT_IDENTITY.md` lift path** (`docs/architecture/FILLET_OUTPUT_IDENTITY.md:42-49`): a focused ADR or dispatch for a cap-face ID scheme covering kind bytes, canonical cap ordering, owner discipline, and topology-lineage interactions.
+>
+> Both lift paths are additive (no cascading substrate changes), so the deferral has no continuing cost.
+
+Because the next step is a policy decision, no allowed-files set, no test plan, no focused/canonical gate list, and no MUST-NOT list for an implementation task is being authored here — per the TASK packet's Deliverables Q4 clause: "If the smallest next step is a policy decision, Q4 states the decision instead of inventing an implementation task."
+
+### Q5. What should task #30 be?
+
+**`NEEDS_HUMAN`**.
+
+Rationale:
+
+- Q3 found zero `consumer-pressure-present` gaps and zero `needs-ADR` gaps; every remaining gap is `pressure-deferred` (waiting on a concrete consumer) or `already-solved` (resolver / label / projection contracts in place).
+- Per the TASK packet halt conditions, "the audit finds that D-Fillet output identity is already fully solved and no bounded product follow-up exists; route to `NEEDS_HUMAN` rather than inventing work" and "the audit finds that the remaining identity choice is product or architecture policy rather than a bounded implementation."
+- Recommending a fresh ADR or doctrine refresh now would itself be premature: ADR-119 and ADR-120 are recent (2026-05-12 / post-2026-05-12) and explicitly establish "wait for pressure" as the doctrine. `FILLET_OUTPUT_IDENTITY.md` already records the open question precisely.
+- The arbitration belongs to a human: deciding whether (and which) consumer is allowed to motivate cap-face identity work — vs. continuing to defer — is a product/architecture choice, not an Executor choice.
+
+`NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+No source/test/doc files were edited. The only new files produced by this dispatch are this EXEC report and the matching sidecar (generated by `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize`).
+
+## Verification Results
+
+Per TASK packet "No cargo, formatter, architecture-lint, or test command is required as an Executor gate for this read-only audit." Repository-state gates were run and are documented under "Worktree State" below.
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` (pre-audit) → empty output (clean tracked tree).
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` (post-audit, after writing this EXEC report) → only this dispatch's own `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-142_EXEC_*` artifacts visible; see Worktree State.
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope. No `crates/**`, `editor/**`, `kernel/**`, `.github/**`, Cargo, script, doctrine, status, ADR, architecture, fixture, or existing-handoff edit occurred. No issue / label / PR / branch / remote-state change was made.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+The Reviewer / arbiter should confirm:
+
+1. There is no internal or external consumer (currently un-checked-in or otherwise hidden from this audit) that needs stable `BRepFaceId`s for chamfer caps, round-fillet cylinder/cap/corner surfaces today. If such a consumer exists, the route should change from `NEEDS_HUMAN` to a focused ADR or implementation dispatch on the consumer's terms.
+2. The Q3 classification of "picking / selection" and "projection / highlight" as `already-solved` is the right read — the current contract (DEGENERATE → transparent → no `BRepFaceId`) is intended, not a defect.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: clean before audit; after writing this EXEC report, the only tracked-change candidates are this dispatch's own `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-142_EXEC_2026-05-24_09-37-44+0300.md` and its `.meta.json` sidecar (created by `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize`).
+- Untracked items: `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-142_TASK_2026-05-24_09-30-22+0300.md` and `.meta.json` (the TASK packet for this dispatch; pre-existing in the working tree before EXEC began, per the initial git-status snapshot in the conversation header).
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-142`
+- Last commit: `48e46b8` chore(dispatch): seed task #29 D-Fillet output-identity preflight
+

+

+- GitHub issue #142
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Perform GitHub issue #142 as a read-only D-Fillet output-identity preflight. The repository no longer has a generic D-Fillet blocker: ADR-119 D1-D8 are recorded as closed, chamfer `FilletOp` has graph-level face inheritance plus filtered edge inheritance, and real `RoundFilletOp` has landed across Cuboid, Extrude, Revolve, and Loft with multi-edge corner handling. This audit must determine what output-identity gap, if any, remains for D-Fillet outputs, especially `RoundFilletOp`'s nameless cylinder, cap, and corner-patch surfaces. The result is a single EXEC report that separates chamfer `FilletOp` identity from `RoundFilletOp` identity, answers Q1-Q5 with file/line evidence, and recommends exactly one bounded task #30 or routes to `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- None. This is an audit-only dispatch.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- Any `crates/**` source or test file.
+- Any `editor/**` file.
+- Any `kernel/**` file.
+- Any `.github/**` workflow file.
+- Any Cargo file, including `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, workspace manifests, generated Cargo metadata, or dependency declarations.
+- Any PowerShell script, shell script, schema, architecture lint, doctrine document, status document, planning document, ADR, architecture document, fixture, generated asset, or root-level repository document.
+- Any existing handoff packet or existing dispatch log, including this TASK packet after execution begins.
+- Any GitHub issue, label, PR, branch, review, comment, commit, remote state, or queue metadata except the queue runner's normal issue lifecycle for this dispatch.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- Exactly one execution report matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-142_EXEC_*.md`.
+- The matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-142_EXEC_*.meta.json` sidecar only if `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize` creates it while finalizing the EXEC report.
+- This dispatch's own queue-runner log under `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` only if the queue runner creates it as part of normal dispatch operation.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New source files, test files, fixtures, docs, architecture docs, generated assets, Cargo files, dependency files, schemas, scripts, workflows, architecture lints, TASK packets, REVIEW packets, CLOSEOUT packets, issue notes, or design documents.
+- Any handoff packet other than this dispatch's single `ISSUE-142_EXEC_*.md` report and optional matching `.meta.json` sidecar.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- One read-only `EXECUTION_REPORT` packet for ISSUE-142.
+- The EXEC report contains a literal `## 5-Question D-Fillet Output-Identity Preflight Answer Block` section.
+- Under that section, the EXEC report contains these exact Q1-Q5 heading lines:
+  - `### Q1. What output identity does chamfer FilletOp have today?`
+  - `### Q2. What output identity does RoundFilletOp have today?`
+  - `### Q3. Which remaining gap is actually load-bearing for the next CAD product step?`
+  - `### Q4. What is the smallest safe follow-up if the gap is bounded?`
+  - `### Q5. What should task #30 be?`
+- Q1 summarizes chamfer `FilletOp` graph-level face inheritance, filtered edge inheritance, direct `BRepProvider` / `BRepEdgeProvider` non-goals, and how chamfer caps are represented in labels/projection, with file/line evidence.
+- Q2 summarizes real `RoundFilletOp` graph-level resolver behavior, inherited faces/edges, nameless cylinder/cap/corner surfaces, `TopologyFaceId::DEGENERATE` usage, and direct provider non-goals, with file/line evidence.
+- Q3 classifies every plausible remaining identity gap as exactly one of `consumer-pressure-present`, `pressure-deferred`, `already-solved`, or `needs-ADR`. Plausible gaps include direct provider impls, stable IDs for generated round surfaces, face-label propagation, edge inheritance behavior, picking and selection, and projection/highlight support.
+- Q4 names exact allowed files, exact tests, focused gates, canonical gates, MUST NOT list, and halt conditions only if Q3 proves the gap is bounded by concrete consumer pressure and existing policy. If the smallest next step is a policy decision, Q4 states the decision instead of inventing an implementation task.
+- Q5 recommends exactly one of: a bounded implementation task, a narrower read-only audit, a docs/doctrine update, or `NEEDS_HUMAN` because the remaining identity choice requires product/architecture arbitration.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The dispatch is read-only except for its own generated EXEC report, optional matching sidecar, and possible queue-runner log.
+- The EXEC report includes the exact five-question block title and exact Q1-Q5 heading lines listed in Deliverables.
+- Q1 and Q2 keep chamfer `FilletOp` identity separate from real `RoundFilletOp` identity and cite current file/line evidence for both.
+- Every remaining gap discussed in Q3 uses one of the required four classifications: `consumer-pressure-present`, `pressure-deferred`, `already-solved`, or `needs-ADR`.
+- The EXEC report must not propose minting stable IDs for generated `RoundFilletOp` cap, cylinder, or corner surfaces unless Q3 shows concrete consumer pressure in current code.
+- The EXEC report must not propose direct provider impls when the graph-level topology resolver already provides the honest identity surface.
+- If the next step requires choosing an identity policy rather than applying an already-bounded implementation, Q5 must end with `NEEDS_HUMAN`; the EXEC report must use `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, include `EXEC_STATUS: blocked`, and end with footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`, `EXIT_CODE: 0`.
+- If a bounded follow-up exists, Q5 must name one task #30 route with exact allowed files, exact tests, exact MUST NOT list, focused gates, canonical gates, and halt conditions. The EXEC report then uses normal successful EXEC routing to the reviewer.
+- The final worktree check confirms no tracked source, test, Cargo, workflow, script, doctrine, status-doc, architecture-doc, existing-packet, issue-label, or other forbidden edit occurred.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not implement any D-Fillet identity change in this dispatch.
+- Do not edit tests or add test coverage in this dispatch.
+- Do not edit ADR-119, architecture docs, `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, `change.md`, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, or any other doctrine/status/planning surface.
+- Do not mint or recommend stable IDs for generated `RoundFilletOp` cylinder, cap, or corner surfaces unless Q3 identifies concrete consumer pressure in current code.
+- Do not add or recommend direct `BRepProvider` / `BRepEdgeProvider` impls if current graph-level resolver/provider behavior is already the correct identity layer.
+- Do not alter tessellation labels, projection labels, topology resolver contracts, provider contracts, operator graph structure, or CAD product policy.
+- Do not infer dispatch completion from missing `_REVIEW_` or `_CLOSEOUT_` packets; this orchestrator dispatch normally writes TASK and EXEC packets only.
+- Do not commit, push, open a PR, create or edit GitHub issues, change labels, or write follow-up packets.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in the `EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` before any audit work. Document all pre-existing tracked changes if any are visible. Do not clean, revert, or modify unrelated user changes.
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` after writing the EXEC report. Confirm that visible tracked changes, if any, are limited to this dispatch's own `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-142_*` packet artifacts and/or this dispatch's own `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact, and that no tracked source, test, Cargo, workflow, script, doctrine, status-doc, architecture-doc, existing-packet, issue-label, or other forbidden file changed.
+
+No cargo, formatter, architecture-lint, or test command is required as an Executor gate for this read-only audit. If the orchestrator or queue runner runs repository verification after EXEC and that verification fails outside this audit's allowed artifact surface, do not fix source or tests; halt/report according to the halt conditions below.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without broadening scope and request fresh guidance if any of the following occur:
+
+- Answering Q1-Q5 requires editing source, tests, Cargo, workflows, scripts, doctrine, status docs, architecture docs, issues, labels, existing packets, or any other forbidden file.
+- The current code cannot be inspected enough to provide file/line evidence for the required chamfer `FilletOp` and `RoundFilletOp` identity findings.
+- The answer would require changing `BRepFaceId`, `BRepEdgeId`, `BRepProvider`, `BRepEdgeProvider`, `TopologyFaceId`, resolver signatures, operator graph structure, or projection contracts before a human chooses the identity policy.
+- The smallest follow-up would mint stable IDs for generated `RoundFilletOp` surfaces without evidence of consumer pressure.
+- The smallest follow-up would add direct provider impls when the graph-level resolver already provides the honest identity surface.
+- The audit finds that D-Fillet output identity is already fully solved and no bounded product follow-up exists; route to `NEEDS_HUMAN` rather than inventing work.
+- The audit finds that the remaining identity choice is product or architecture policy rather than a bounded implementation. Complete the five-question block and route to `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+- Any required verification or repository-state check shows tracked changes outside this dispatch's own allowed handoff/log artifacts.
+- Any forbidden edit or forbidden artifact is created. Stop immediately, do not expand the diff, and report the deviation in the EXEC report.
+- A correction prompt asks the Executor to fix any source/test/Cargo/workflow/script/doc/status failure for this read-only audit. Do not execute that correction; write or update the allowed EXEC report with `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, `EXEC_STATUS: blocked`, footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`, and `EXIT_CODE: 0`.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+This dispatch comes directly from `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` task #29 and GitHub issue #142. It is a D-Fillet output-identity preflight, not a D-Fillet implementation task. The audit should read ADR-119, current fillet and round-fillet CAD code, topology resolvers/providers, cad-projection surfaces, focused tests, and status history. Use historical files as evidence only; do not edit them.
+
+The central discipline is consumer pressure. Chamfer `FilletOp` and real `RoundFilletOp` have different identity surfaces and must not be collapsed into a single "fillet identity" answer. Generated `RoundFilletOp` cylinder, cap, and corner surfaces are especially sensitive: do not recommend minting stable IDs for them unless current code shows a concrete consumer that needs those IDs now. If the remaining gap is an identity policy choice, the correct route is `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+
+Under the orchestrator protocol, no `_REVIEW_` or `_CLOSEOUT_` packet is expected from this TASK. The only new packet produced by execution should be the `ISSUE-142_EXEC_*.md` report, plus its optional generated sidecar and the queue runner's own log if applicable.
